Schnittstelle IMulti

Alle Superschnittstellen:
BankTree.IBankObject
public interface IMulti extends BankTree.IBankObject
Implementations represent one synthesizer multi. A multi features a number of parts to which patches can be assigned.
Autor:
Matthias Wolff

  • Methodenübersicht

    Modifizierer und Typ
    Methode
    Beschreibung
    float
    getDetune(int part)
    Gets the detuning of a part.
    int
    getMidiChannel(int part)
    Returns the MIDI channel assigned to a multi part.
    String
    getName()
    Returns the name of the multi.
    int
    getNumberOfParts()
    Returns the number of multi parts.
    float
    getPan(int part)
    Gets the panning of a part
    String
    getPatchBankUID(int part)
    Returns the unique patch bank identifier of the patch assigned to a multi part.
    String
    getPatchUID(int part)
    Returns the unique identifier of the patch assigned to a multi part.
    default int
    getPolyphony(int part)
    Returns the number of voices fixedly assigned to a multi part.
    default int
    getReserveNotes(int part)
    Returns the number of voices reserved for a multi part.
    float
    getVolume(int part)
    Gets the volume of a part.
    boolean
    isPartActive(int part)
    Determines whether a multi part is on or off.
    void
    setDetune(int part, float detune)
    Sets the detuning of a part.
    default void
    setMidiChannel(int part, int channel)
    Sets the MIDI channel assigned to a multi part.
    void
    setName(String name)
    Sets the name of the multi.
    default void
    setPan(int part, float pan)
    Gets the panning of a part
    default void
    setPartActive(int part, boolean active)
    Switches a multi part on or off.
    void
    setPatch(int part, String bankUID, String patchUID)
    Assigns a patch to a multi part.
    default void
    setPolyphony(int part, int polyphony)
    Sets the the number of voices fixedly assigned to a multi part.
    default void
    setReserveNotes(int part, int reserveNotes)
    Sets the number of voices reserved for a multi part.
    void
    setVolume(int part, float volume)
    Sets the volume of a part.
    boolean
    supportsPan()
    Determines whether the synthesizer supports panning.
    boolean
    supportsPolyphony()
    Determines whether the synthesizer supports fixed voice allocation.
    boolean
    supportsReserveNotes()
    Determines whether the synthesizer supports reserving a number of voices for each multi part.
    boolean
    supportsSetMidiChannel()
    Determines whether the synthesizer supports assigning a MIDI channel to each multi part.
    boolean
    supportsSetPartActive()
    Determines whether the synthesizer supports switching multi parts off.

  • Methodendetails

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns the name of the multi.
      Angegeben von:
      getName in Schnittstelle BankTree.IBankObject
      Siehe auch:
      • setName(String)

    • setName

      void setName(String name)
      Sets the name of the multi.
      Parameter:
      name - The name
      Siehe auch:
      • getName()

    • getNumberOfParts

      int getNumberOfParts()
      Returns the number of multi parts.

    • getPatchBankUID

      String getPatchBankUID(int part) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the unique patch bank identifier of the patch assigned to a multi part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The unique patch bank identifier
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • getPatchUID(int)
      • setPatch(int, String, String)

    • getPatchUID

      String getPatchUID(int part) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the unique identifier of the patch assigned to a multi part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The unique identifier of the patch in its patch bank
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • getPatchBankUID(int)
      • setPatch(int, String, String)

    • setPatch

      void setPatch(int part, String bankUID, String patchUID) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Assigns a patch to a multi part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      bankUID - The unique patch bank identifier of the patch to set
      patchUID - The unique identifier of the patch to set in the patch bank identified by bankUID
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if
      • if part is out of range, or
      • if bankUID is null or empty, or if no such bank exists, or
      • if patchUID is null or empty, or if no such patch exists in the patch bank identified by bankUID
      Siehe auch:
      • getPatchBankUID(int)
      • getPatchUID(int)

    • supportsSetPartActive

      boolean supportsSetPartActive()
      Determines whether the synthesizer supports switching multi parts off.
      Siehe auch:
      • isPartActive(int)
      • setPartActive(int, boolean)

    • isPartActive

      boolean isPartActive(int part) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Determines whether a multi part is on or off.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsSetPartActive()
      • setPartActive(int, boolean)

    • setPartActive

      default void setPartActive(int part, boolean active) throws NotSupportedException, IllegalArgumentException
      Switches a multi part on or off.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      active - true to switch the part on, false to switch the part off
      Löst aus:
      NotSupportedException - if the synthesizer does not support switching parts off
      IllegalArgumentException - if part is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsSetPartActive()
      • isPartActive(int)

    • supportsSetMidiChannel

      boolean supportsSetMidiChannel()
      Determines whether the synthesizer supports assigning a MIDI channel to each multi part.
      Siehe auch:
      • getMidiChannel(int)
      • setMidiChannel(int, int)

    • getMidiChannel

      int getMidiChannel(int part) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the MIDI channel assigned to a multi part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The one-based MIDI channel ∈ [1,16]
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsSetMidiChannel()
      • setMidiChannel(int, int)

    • setMidiChannel

      default void setMidiChannel(int part, int channel) throws NotSupportedException, IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the MIDI channel assigned to a multi part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      channel - The one-based MIDI channel ∈ [1,16]
      Löst aus:
      NotSupportedException - if the synthesizer does not support assigning a MIDI channel to each multi part
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsSetMidiChannel()
      • getMidiChannel(int)

    • supportsPolyphony

      boolean supportsPolyphony()
      Determines whether the synthesizer supports fixed voice allocation. Fixed voice allocation—in contrast to dynamic voice allocation—allows to assign a fix number of voices to a multi part.
      Siehe auch:
      • getPolyphony(int)
      • setPolyphony(int, int)
      • supportsReserveNotes()

    • getPolyphony

      default int getPolyphony(int part) throws NotSupportedException, IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the number of voices fixedly assigned to a multi part. The default implementation throws a NotSupportedException.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The number of voices assigned to part
      Löst aus:
      NotSupportedException - if the synthesizer does not support fixed voice allocation
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsPolyphony()
      • setPolyphony(int, int)

    • setPolyphony

      default void setPolyphony(int part, int polyphony) throws NotSupportedException, InvalidMidiDataException, IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the the number of voices fixedly assigned to a multi part. The default implementation throws a NotSupportedException.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      polyphony - The number of voices to assign to the part. Note that the greatest permissible value typically depends on the polyphony settings of the other parts.
      Löst aus:
      NotSupportedException - if the synthesizer does not support fixed voice allocation
      InvalidMidiDataException - if the number of voice specified by polyphony cannot be assigned
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range.
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsPolyphony()
      • getPolyphony(int)

    • supportsReserveNotes

      boolean supportsReserveNotes()
      Determines whether the synthesizer supports reserving a number of voices for each multi part. Synthesizers allowing reserving notes feature dynamic voice allocation, i.e., they can automatically assign voices to multi parts as needed.
      Siehe auch:
      • getReserveNotes(int)
      • setReserveNotes(int, int)
      • supportsPolyphony()

    • getReserveNotes

      default int getReserveNotes(int part) throws NotSupportedException, IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the number of voices reserved for a multi part. The default implementation throws a NotSupportedException.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The number of voices reserved for part. The return value 0 typically indicates automatic voice assignment.
      Löst aus:
      NotSupportedException - if the synthesizer does not support reserving notes
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsReserveNotes()
      • setReserveNotes(int, int)

    • setReserveNotes

      default void setReserveNotes(int part, int reserveNotes) throws NotSupportedException, InvalidMidiDataException, IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the number of voices reserved for a multi part. The default implementation throws a NotSupportedException.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      reserveNotes - The number of voices to reserve for part. The value 0 typically indicates automatic voice assignment.
      Löst aus:
      NotSupportedException - if the synthesizer does not support reserving notes
      InvalidMidiDataException - if the number of voice specified by reserveNotes cannot be reserved
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range.
      Siehe auch:
      • supportsReserveNotes()
      • getReserveNotes(int)

    • getVolume

      float getVolume(int part) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Gets the volume of a part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The volume ∈ [0,1]
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range

    • setVolume

      void setVolume(int part, float volume) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the volume of a part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      volume - The volume ∈ [0,1]
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range

    • getDetune

      float getDetune(int part) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Gets the detuning of a part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The detuning in semitones. The decimal places denote fine detuning in cents. Detuning can be positive or negative; zero means no detuning.
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range
      Siehe auch:
      • Ungültige Referenz 
        SYX#encodeDetune(String, int, int)

    • setDetune

      void setDetune(int part, float detune) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the detuning of a part.
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      detune - The detuning in semitones. The decimal places denote fine detuning in cents. Detuning can be positive or negative; zero means no detuning.
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range. Note that the permissible detuning range depends on the synthesizer.
      Siehe auch:
      • Ungültige Referenz 
        SYX#encodeDetune(String, int, int)

    • supportsPan

      boolean supportsPan()
      Determines whether the synthesizer supports panning.

    • getPan

      float getPan(int part) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Gets the panning of a part
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      Gibt zurück:
      The panning ∈ [-1,+1] where -1 means maximum left and +1 denotes maximum right
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument is out of range

    • setPan

      default void setPan(int part, float pan) throws NotSupportedException, IllegalArgumentException
      Gets the panning of a part
      Parameter:
      part - The zero-based part number ∈ [0,getNumberOfParts()-1]
      pan - The panning ∈ [-1,+1] where -1 means maximum left and +1 denotes maximum right
      Löst aus:
      NotSupportedException - if the synthesizer does not support panning
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range
